titleStmt.title: The Secretary of State to the Ambassador in China ( (Gauss))

div.head: The Secretary of State to the Ambassador in China ( Gauss )
div.p:
569. The Department has not communicated your 721, April 24, 4 p.m., to the War Department but it is believed advisable that you suggest to General Hearn that, if he has not already done so, he communicate with the War Department in regard to the matter. If you deem it advisable you might again speak to General Hearn along the lines of the final paragraph of your telegram suggesting that a more official and direct method be devised for communicating messages from the President to the Generalissimo than that which has been customarily employed; that obviously the preferable method would be the personal delivery of such messages to the Generalissimo himself; and that, in consideration of the fact that the Generalissimo does not understand English, any subsequent message from the President to Generalissimo Chiang be accompanied by a careful translation into Chinese (you might offer the facilities of the Embassy for this purpose). In this latter connection, Department believes that, all phases of the matter considered, it is advisable that the Generalissimo have
an Embassy (or U. S. Army) translation into Chinese of important messages from the President.
